notes: Irene Adler and Godfrey Norton marry during the events of A Scandal in Bohemia — with Sherlock Holmes as an unwitting witness to the ceremony. They depart England for the Continent immediately after, rendering Holmes's mission moot and placing both characters beyond the reach of any further intrigue. The marriage is presented as genuine rather than tactical: they had already been meeting regularly before the ceremony, and Norton's urgency to marry suggests he feared Adler's safety more than he feared delay. Watson notes it in passing; Holmes, characteristically, does not comment on it directly.
